DEFENDANT MUST PRODUCE THE COMPUTER AND BACK UP DATA, EVEN IF IT NEEDS TO IMMEDIATELY REPURCHASE THE COMPUTER AND RESTORE THE HARD DRIVE AT ITS OWN EXPENSE. This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: plaintiff's motion to quash notice of deposition of plaintiff's counsel and for protective order is granted. Defendant has not met its burden under Carehouse/Spectra-Physics. Further, the court denied the request for sanctions. Order signed. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

Off Calendar Aug-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JOHN KAO'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF COMPUTERS AND BACKUP DATA FOR EXAMINATION.
Appearances: Linda K. Adler of the Vartain Law Group (391.1155) for defendants and responding parties; Christopher W. Katzenbach of Katzenbach and Khtikian (834.1778) for plaintiff and moving party.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: plaintiff's motion to compel production of computers and back up data of Jennifer Turpin's computer is granted. Defendant must produce the computer and back up data, even if it needs to immediately repurchase the computer and restore the hard drive at its own expense. If the computer, hard drive or information contained therein cannot be recovered the plaintiff should bring a motion for sanctions. Further, the court grants defendant's request for a protective order, which was stipulated to by the parties. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MARTHA PEUGH-WADE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Christopher W. Katzenbach of Katzenbach and Khtikian (834.1778) for plaintiff and responding party; Michael Vartain of the Vartain Law Group (no card provided) for defendants and moving parties. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion for summary judgment is denied, summary adjudication is denied. As to the 1st, 2nd, and 5th causes of action, plaintiff raises triable issues of fact whether U.S.F. was really concerned about plaintiff's fitness to perform his job, and whether the examination was sought because of business needs. As to the 3rd and 4th causes of action, a triable issue of fact is raised as to what information was being requested by U.S.F. The letters to plaintiff and to Dr. Reynolds, on their face, arguably request more information than what is allowed. As to the 6th cause of action, a triable issue of fact is raised whether Ms. Peugh-Wade acted with malice, or without reasonable belief in the truth of the statements made. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
